scenario: Ridge Telco runs Camunda 8.8 with 14 Spring Boot services that maintain long-lived gRPC channels and handle 23000 jobs per hour. The platform team configures camunda.client.zeebe.keep-alive in its YAML and must migrate to the documented 8.8 replacement before retiring the legacy key.
question: Which property does the 8.8 reference list as the replacement for the legacy camunda.client.zeebe.keep-alive key?

options.text: The 8.8 reference replaces camunda.client.zeebe.keep-alive with camunda.client.keep-alive at the unified camunda.client level of the Spring Boot starter.
The 8.8 reference replaces camunda.client.zeebe.keep-alive with camunda.client.grpc.keep-alive at the unified camunda.client level of the Spring Boot starter.
The 8.8 reference replaces camunda.client.zeebe.keep-alive with camunda.client.broker.keep-alive at the unified camunda.client level of the Spring Boot starter.
The 8.8 reference replaces camunda.client.zeebe.keep-alive with camunda.client.auth.keep-alive at the unified camunda.client level of the Spring Boot starter.

a.text: Correct. The camunda.client.zeebe table points keep-alive to camunda.client.keep-alive as the documented 8.8 replacement key under the unified prefix.

b.text: Incorrect. The reference does not introduce a camunda.client.grpc subsection; keep-alive sits directly under camunda.client in 8.8.

c.text: Incorrect. The unified replacement drops the broker segment, so camunda.client.broker.keep-alive is not the listed replacement key.

d.text: Incorrect. keep-alive is a transport setting, not an OAuth setting; the documented replacement lives under camunda.client directly.

explanation: camunda.client.zeebe.keep-alive migrates to camunda.client.keep-alive in the 8.8 reference. The property tunes the gRPC keep-alive interval on the unified client.

docs.title: Camunda Spring Boot starter properties reference
